metric_value: DB2 for MVS costs too much
notes: Direct Aberdeen assessment: DB2 for MVS cited as excessively costly relative to competitive alternatives.

metric_value: DB2/6000 on SMP suffers from weak RS/6000 SMP story
notes: IBM RS/6000 SMP platform weakness cited as dragging DB2/6000 SMP positioning.

metric_value: DB2/2 PE on SP credible but lags Informix
notes: DB2 Parallel Edition on IBM SP is competitive but ranked below Informix XPS on SP2.

metric_value: Strength: query optimizer and multimedia query
notes: DB2 query optimizer cited as a key strength alongside multimedia query capability.

metric_value: Do not believe the cross-platform story
notes: Aberdeen advises skepticism of IBM's cross-platform claims for DB2.

metric_value: DB2/6000 does not have attention/understanding of IBM sales force
notes: IBM's own sales force cited as a weakness: insufficient focus and understanding of DB2/6000.

metric_value: Competitive but not leading on SMP
notes: DB2 for AIX rated competitive but not best-in-class on SMP scalability.

metric_value: Only compatible SMP and MPP story except NCR
notes: IBM DB2 cited as one of only two vendors (with NCR) offering compatible SMP+MPP architecture.

metric_value: Good async replication in Data Propagator; no peer-peer replication
notes: DB2 Data Propagator rated good for async replication but absent on peer-to-peer replication.

metric_value: Full 2-way DRDA and ODBC; limited non-IBM gateways
notes: DB2 for AIX rated as best choice in IBM-homogeneous environment but limited outside IBM stack.

metric_value: More limited ISV tool selection than Informix/Oracle/Sybase; tools improving
notes: DB2 development tool ecosystem rated weaker than the three principal rivals.

metric_value: Fast moving DB2 into searchable audio/video/user-defined data types
notes: DB2 extenders (text/image/audio/video) cited as rapidly evolving toward Universal Server capability.

metric_value: Informix wins most head-to-head benchmarks it competes in
notes: Aberdeen notes Informix has strong track record winning competitive benchmarks.

metric_value: XPS architecture widely praised for elegance
notes: Aberdeen notes broad market appreciation for Informix XPS (Extended Parallel Server) architecture.

metric_value: Good scalability to 8 processors on SMP; many TPC-Cs
notes: Informix OnLine 7 rated strong on SMP scalability with multiple TPC-C benchmark credentials.

metric_value: Now on SP2 and other loosely-coupled HW with version 8 for data warehousing
notes: Informix OnLine 8/XPS now supports IBM SP2 and loose-coupled MPP hardware for data warehousing.

metric_value: Financial stumble in 1997 hurts credibility
notes: Informix's 1997 financial/accounting difficulties cited as damaging enterprise buyer confidence.

metric_value: Limited resources and customer base
notes: Informix rated as constrained by smaller resource base and customer count vs. Oracle/IBM.

metric_value: Limited visibility/experience at CIO level
notes: Informix lacks the CIO-level brand recognition and executive relationships of Oracle and IBM.

metric_value: No-consulting policy slowed large enterprise growth
notes: Informix's policy of not offering consulting services historically constrained large-enterprise adoption.

metric_value: Behind in replication; fair in gateways
notes: Informix rated below market leaders on replication capability and only fair on gateway breadth.

metric_value: Enterprise-class; positioned for large enterprises
notes: Oracle's stated 1998 positioning: enterprise-class database for large enterprise customers.

metric_value: Products seldom lead in technology but are robust and enterprise-capable
notes: Aberdeen rates Oracle products as rarely first-to-market on features but reliable for enterprise use.

metric_value: Good replication including peer-peer; evolving network computing model
notes: Oracle rated strong on distributed replication (including peer-to-peer) and progressing on network computing.

metric_value: Tools in transition; OO failed in 1997
notes: Oracle development tool portfolio in mid-transition; 1997 OO tools effort cited as failure.

metric_value: Most aggressive in multimedia marketing; light objects in Oracle8
notes: Oracle rated most aggressive in marketing Universal Server/multimedia but Oracle8 object support still lightweight.

metric_value: RDBMS engine looks dated; is v7.3 a TPC-C stop-gap or major re-write?
notes: Aberdeen questions whether Oracle 7.3 represents meaningful re-architecture or only benchmark optimization.

metric_value: No 2nd-generation client-server tools; so-so on Windows
notes: Oracle lacks competitive second-generation client-server development tools and has weak Windows positioning.

metric_value: Service/consulting drive makes ISV partnering tough
notes: Oracle's aggressive services push competes with its own ISV ecosystem, straining partner relations.

metric_value: Oracle is beatable by focused selling
notes: Aberdeen direct guidance to CA sales force: Oracle's hit-and-run quota-driven reps are vulnerable to focused competitive selling.

metric_value: 1980s architecture runs out of steam; needs System 11 now
notes: Sybase SQL Server architecture characterized as generationally obsolete; System 11 needed urgently.

metric_value: Poor scalability in SMP
notes: Sybase rated poor on SMP scalability — a key weakness heading into 1998.

metric_value: Navigation Server platform-limited to NCR 3600; SP2 version imminent
notes: Sybase Navigation Server MPP product locked to single hardware platform (NCR 3600) at study date.

metric_value: PowerBuilder is a market leader in dev tools
notes: Sybase/Powersoft PowerBuilder cited as market-leading client-server development tool.

metric_value: System 11 improves scalability markedly; good on uniprocessor
notes: System 11 upgrade delivers meaningful scalability gains; uniprocessor performance strong.

metric_value: Best gateway breadth overall; most flexible
notes: Sybase rated top among all vendors for gateway breadth and overall openness.

metric_value: Momentum is downwards but could recover in 1996
notes: Aberdeen notes negative market momentum for Sybase (note: text says 1996, likely reflecting data lag vs. 1997 publication).

metric_value: Modest scalability; no MPP; good on DEC Alpha
notes: Microsoft SQL Server 6.5 rated only modestly scalable with no MPP path; performs well on DEC Alpha.

metric_value: Limited replication in SQL Server 6.5 for NT
notes: Microsoft SQL Server 6.5 rated limited on distributed replication capability.

metric_value: Great for PC apps; OLE; ODBC
notes: Microsoft rated strong on PC/Windows openness (ODBC/OLE) but limited in enterprise-class open standards.

metric_value: Major language supplier; ISV leader
notes: Microsoft cited as the leading ISV ecosystem and programming language supplier.

metric_value: Greater than 13000 TPC-C on NT
notes: SQL Server 7 benchmark result: >13,000 TPC-C transactions/minute on Windows NT.

metric_value: Highly aggressive pricing
notes: SQL Server 7 positioned with highly aggressive pricing strategy vs. enterprise RDBMS incumbents.

metric_value: Behind in universal server, data warehousing, clustering
notes: SQL Server 6.5 cited as lagging on Universal Server extensions, data warehousing, and clustering vs. Unix-based rivals.

metric_value: RDBMS architecture is behind — no parallel/scalable
notes: CA-Ingres lacks parallel or scalable RDBMS architecture; trailing all major competitors on this dimension.

metric_value: No Ingres people remain — but few customers have defected
notes: Original Ingres engineering team departed; CA retention of customers noted despite this.

metric_value: CA sales force does not understand the Unix world well
notes: Structural sales force weakness: insufficient Unix expertise for RDBMS competitive selling.

metric_value: Product quality was poor; now much better
notes: Historical product quality problems acknowledged; improvement noted at study date.

metric_value: OPENROAD left customers with character 4GL behind — incompatible environment
notes: CA OPENROAD introduced an incompatible new environment relative to existing character-based 4GL; created customer migration friction.

metric_value: CA-Ingres is number four in RDBMS and trailing
notes: Aberdeen explicitly characterizes CA-Ingres as the fourth-place RDBMS vendor; difficult competitive position.

metric_value: TPC-D equals decision support — industry theme
notes: TPC-D benchmark cited as 1997 industry theme equating to decision support marketing positioning.

metric_value: Object navigation will become desirable for some apps such as Jasmine
notes: Aberdeen conclusion: object navigation is approaching practical relevance; CA Jasmine cited as exemplar.

metric_value: Almost half of Oracle revenues are services
notes: Aberdeen notes Oracle's service/consulting revenues approach 50% of total — structural issue for ISV partners.

metric_value: Best ORDBMS framework in DataBlades; same searchable data types as IBM
notes: Aberdeen rates Informix IUS/DataBlade as the leading ORDBMS extensibility framework in the market.
